result.summary: Apigee on apigee.googleapis.com exposes a REST management API, a public MCP endpoint at https://apigee.googleapis.com/mcp, and a gcloud apigee CLI; the API and CLI use Google Cloud OAuth access tokens.
result.description: Apigee is Google Cloud’s API management platform for developing, securing, publishing, and operating APIs. It provides control-plane APIs and tooling for managing Apigee organizations, environments, proxies, products, and related resources.

google_oauth.label: Google Cloud OAuth access token
google_oauth.setup: For Apigee API and `gcloud` access, use a Google Cloud identity and authenticate with the Google Cloud CLI. The easiest path is to install the [Google Cloud CLI](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install) and run [`gcloud auth login`](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/login) for a user account, or configure a service account with [`gcloud auth activate-service-account`](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/activate-service-account). The Apigee getting started docs state that the Apigee API supports OAuth 2.0 for user authentication and use `gcloud` credentials for examples.

evidence: Seed fact: MCP server endpoint: https://apigee.googleapis.com/mcp (auth: none)
